title: The City Where We Fell in Love
content: Whenever people ask why I still visit that city every autumn, I usually mention the architecture, the museums, or the cafés along the river. The truth is much simpler. It was the place where we first learned how ordinary days could become unforgettable. Every street corner carries a memory. The bookstore where we spent rainy afternoons. The bridge where we watched sunsets without speaking. The tiny bakery whose owner always remembered our order. The city itself has continued changing. New buildings have appeared, old shops have disappeared, and unfamiliar faces now fill the streets. Yet every visit reminds me that places do not preserve memories on their own. They borrow meaning from the people who once laughed, dreamed, and loved within them.
